title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban ahead of 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban is ranked No. 32 in ESPN's draft prospects list and is a Southborough native.
Karaban played 151 games at UConn, setting records for wins, games played, and three-pointers made.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway, mixing jazz and pop in his 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour.'
Puth shared nostalgic Boston memories as a Berklee alum and kept the set 'extra jazzy' to match his 'Professor Puth' persona.
Openers Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey showcased musical talent, with Seavey surprising fans by creating live loops onstage.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers played their 1996 album 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full during a sold-out show in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan and the band followed the album with Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' as part of their concert setlist.
Encore included Wallflowers' songs from 'Breach' and 'Red Letter Days' and covers of Petty's 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting.'

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston experienced the state's largest population decline, losing 1,338 residents last year, according to U.S. Census data.
Everett and Worcester saw the biggest population gains in Massachusetts, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
Housing costs and reduced immigration are major factors in Boston's population decrease, slowing overall Massachusetts growth to 0.2%.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Demoulas Super Markets were listed among America's largest family-run businesses by Forbes.
Fidelity, ranked 14th, reported $37.7 billion in revenue, while Demoulas, ranked 69th, reported $8 billion.
Despite owning less than 50%, Fidelity's leadership by Abigail Johnson qualifies it as a family business.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major campus housing overhaul to expand and modernize student living.
The housing project aims to enhance residential life, maintain affordability, and improve dining and retail amenities on campus.
University officials plan a phased redevelopment, allowing campus continuity while upgrading older buildings and addressing housing shortages.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre, Medford, showcasing songs from her new album 'World's Gone Wrong' with themes of protest.
Williams committed to a politically charged setlist, including eight songs from her latest and 2020's 'Good Souls Better Angels'.
The concert included covers of Bob Marley's 'So Much Trouble in the World' and Neil Young's 'Rockin' in the Free World'.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle had a solid start with nine strikeouts before being relieved after six innings.
Justin Slaten struggled in the seventh, allowing four hits, four runs, and two homers without finishing the inning.
The Red Sox offense scored six runs, but the Twins ultimately defeated Boston 8-6 at Fenway Park.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the ceremonial first pitch at Fenway Park to his son, Kody Clemens, a Minnesota Twins player.
Roger Clemens, a Red Sox legend, returned to Fenway Park at age 63 for the ceremonial pitch on a bobblehead giveaway night.
Kody Clemens, in his second season with the Twins, previously played for the Detroit Tigers and Philadelphia Phillies.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to NBA All-Defensive First Team for first time in 2025-26 with 58 votes.
White recorded 98 blocks and 88 steals, becoming the fourth Celtic with those numbers in franchise history.
White's defense improved Boston's performance by 7.4 points per 100 possessions while leading all guards in contested shots.
